observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Management Office (CBMO) and CAICO to use the OMOE account only for expenditures that cannot be classified under specific asset or expense accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction:
The City Budget Officer informed the Audit Team that they had already made adjustments during budget preparations for CY 2025. They will monitor the classification of these accounts to minimize and eventually prevent the recurrence of deficiencies and misclassifications. They have committed to using the OMOE account exclusively for expenditures that cannot be appropriately classified under specific asset or expense accounts in accordance with the COA Circular. Lastly, they will ensure that expenses are properly budgeted in the respective expense account in the next proceedings of budget preparation.
On the other hand, the City Accountant will review the transaction with regard to transfer cost and registration of titles for the purchase of lot amounting to P370,519.07 and will make the necessary adjustment to recognize the capitalization to the Land account by drawing Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.reason:
The CBMO and CAICO were not able to adjust the budget codes and account codes immediately since they have to conduct a thorough review of these accounts before making the adjustments.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 4, Page Nos. 54 to 57, Part II of this Report.
For preparation of Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. CAICO to:
Reverse the derecognition of PPEs totaling P12,167,429.81 made under JEV No. 101-2023-J0000247 dated December 29, 2023, and refrain from derecognizing from the books of accounts missing PPE items without specific authority from the COA; Prepare necessary accounting entries to recognize the Land in the GF of P4,469,000.00, Medical Equipment of P21,683.24, and Technical and Scientific Equipment of P86,480.62 in the Special Education Fund (SEF);
Reconcile the Lapsing Schedule and identify the PPEs with under recording of Accumulated Depreciation and effect the necessary adjusting entries; and recorded PPE Accumulated Depreciation and planning to computerize the monitoring process for all PPE account balances.
b. CGSO to:
Prepare the PCs. Ensure that all issued PPEs are covered with Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) and renew every three years or as necessary.
c. CGSO and the CAICO to:
Complete the reconciliation of their records based on Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) submitted by the Inventory Committee and follow the procedures provided under Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the missing School Building of P2,376,500.04, Office Equipment of P21,195,226.18, ICT of P55,821,030.55, Communication Equipment of P64,675.24, Furniture and Fixtures of P27,939,970.22, all under the SEF totaling P107,397,402.23.
observations.managementAction: The CAICO already adjusted and reversed as evidenced by JEV No. 101-2024-J000039 dated February 29, 2024, and refrain from derecognizing from the books of accounts without specific authority from the COA; The CAICO made adjustments and reversed as evidenced by JEV No. 101-2024-J000040 dated February 29, 2024. The CAICO is currently in the process of conducting reconciliation and planning to computerize the monitoring of all PPE account balances. The CGSO prepared PARs for all issued PPEs. The CGSO and CAICO are continuously conducting reconciliation of their records based on RPCPPE. For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason:
The CAICO started to adjust and reverse some items and has made specific correcting JEVs; however, the reconciliation and full implementation (including computerization and full reconciliation for SEF) has not been completed due to the substantial volume of transactions which necessitated a more extended period for thorough verification.
Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 46 to 50, Part II of this Report.
For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering and Development Department (CEIDD) and engineering and infrastructure proposed project departments to find ways to minimize/avoid delays in project implementation by performing complete/adequate surveys and site investigations prior to the bidding of the project.
observations.managementAction: The CEIDD conducted proposed project investigations and surveys for every proposed project prior to the bidding of the project. They also coordinated with the engineering departments that have proposed the concerned activities being passed over, resulting in unavoidable stoppage of work prior to budget allotment and termination. Hence, the CEIDD and the CPDO enhanced the monitoring and implementation of the PPAs through planning and scheduling, including establishment of detailed timelines with specific milestones and deadlines to track progress and ensure timely completion.
observations.reason: There are instances in which the projects are already budgeted by other departments and considered as priorities for implementation. Hence, ongoing activities are being passed over, resulting in stoppage of work or, worse, termination.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Establish a well-defined policy/guidelines for the selection of applicants/grantees, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and clear-cut procedures for the distribution of limited resources to those in need; and
b. Leverage a computerized system to monitor the grant of financial assistance, streamline processes, enhance transparency and ensure equitable distribution of limited resources.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently in the process of crafting the policies to rationalize the distribution of financial assistance, with varying amounts allocated across contradicting categories, contradicting uniformity. Additionally, the Management is developing a digital system that shall monitor the granting of assistance in the future. They are also in the process of crafting policies to rationalize the distribution and committed to the development of the digital system after the establishment of the clear-cut policy.
observations.reason:
The development of the digital system has not yet started since the establishment of the clear-cut policy has to be completed first.
These findings highlight inconsistencies and call for a reassessment of the allocation process to ensure fairness and transparency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resources and Management Office (CHRMO) to:
a. Develop a staffing pattern plan that outlines the necessary positions, required skills, and competencies for each department/office;
b. Draft and implement written policies and procedures for the engagement of JO/COS based on existing laws and regulations; and
c. Assess the performance/qualifications of the COS/JOs for the possibility of hiring them as permanent employees to fill the 215 vacant positions. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a resolution institutionalizing the engagement of JO/COS services prepared by CHRMO upon review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The CHRMO had formulated policies and is currently finalizing the required staffing pattern plan and policies. They sought the approval of the City Mayor to consolidate the approval of all loan products from various institutions of their Office, and requested that the CCEMPC first obtain pre-approval from their Office before processing and/or granting loans. The CHRMO is also in the process of developing a medium/long-term Human Resource Development Plan (HRMDP), which will serve as the City's guide in implementing various human resource systems. The CHRMO is currently assessing the qualifications of COS/JO for possible absorption as permanent employees. However, the resolution institutionalizing the engagement has not yet been passed.
observations.reason: The CHRMO is still finalizing the policy and assessment; the resolution institutionalizing the engagement of JO/COS has not been passed since the formulation of the policy and assessment must be completed first.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAICO coordinate with the CHRMO to process and update the JO and COS PhilHealth membership records and ensure all contributions to PhilHealth are remitted in full within the prescribed period, following the New Payment Schedule for Premium Contributions set forth by PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: The CAICO assured that all deductions of PhilHealth contributions from employees’ and workers’ salaries are remitted to PhilHealth. They will coordinate with the CHRMO to ensure proper deductions of PhilHealth contributions, thereby preventing the accumulation of amounts in the Due to PhilHealth account. The CHRMO is establishing a mechanism and procedure to assist the CAICO in processing remittances of PhilHealth Contributions and assigned an employee to oversee the process.
observations.reason: There are still unremitted contributions in CY 2024. CAICO conducted review and decreased unremitted amount by 12.30 percent from CY 2023 to CY 2024; however, full compliance is still pending due to JO/COS record issues and necessary updates to membership records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and City GSO to:

a. Secure relevant documents that will establish the completeness and existence
of PPE; and
b. Update and regularly reconcile their respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property
Cards in accordance with Sections 5.12 and 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:

a. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the unrecognized and error in the
computation of depreciation on depreciable PPEs amounting to P7,711,698.22
and P10,309,800.59, respectively, by debiting the Government Equity account
and crediting the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation accounts;

b. Recompute all the depreciation expenses incurred presented in the PPE lapsing
schedule for current and prior years to ensure the accuracy and valuation of
PPE and its corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts in the Financial
Statements and make the necessary adjustments; and

c. Ensure that depreciation expenses are properly calculated in compliance with
Philippine Application Guidance Nos. 3 and 4 of IPSAS 17.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Management implement an integrated
accounting system to automate the calculation process of depreciation to reduce
the risk of human error and ensure consistency.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to:

a. Update its Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and
furnish the CAICO with the duly certified list showing the names of all
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the current year and every
year thereafter to enable the CAICO to correctly set-up the RPT and SET
Receivable for the current and succeeding year; and

b. Coordinate with the City Accounting and Internal Control Office and City
Assessor’s Office to reconcile the noted discrepancy totaling P116,691,386.10
between the CAICO’s records and total delinquency reported in the Certified
List of Delinquent Taxpayers and taxes due and make the necessary
adjustments.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to:

a. Communicate with the LBP-Calamba City Hall Branch, for possible
identification of the noted Credit Memo and Debit Memo in the amounts of
P1,494,021.05 and P4,043.20, respectively, to support the preparation of
adjustments needed for the identified reconciling items;

b. Upon confirming with the bank, secure the supporting documents to ensure the
accuracy and validity of unreconciled deposits in the amount of P4,044,513.21,
for its proper recording in the books of accounts maintained by the City
Accountant and in the cashbooks maintained by the City Treasurer; and

c. Ensure all reconciling items reflected in the BRS were properly supported with
documents.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the
CBMO and CAICO to use the OMOE account only for expenditures that cannot be
classified under specific asset or expense accounts in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CAICO to record the
capitalization of costs related to the transfer, registration and titling of lots for the
land acquired by the City in CY 2024 totaling P370,519.07 in compliance with
IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to reclassify
the portion of the long-term loan due for settlement beyond one year from the
reporting date as non-current liabilities to ensure the fair presentation of the
account in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. Analyze the Accounts Payable outstanding for more than two years and revert
to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to the approval of
the local sanggunian, those accounts payable that are with no actual claims,
administrative or judicial, or which are not covered with perfected contracts on
record; and

b. Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable so that the
account will consist only of valid claims against the City Government.

We also recommend that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to verify the
validity of payables aged more than two years and send confirmation letters for
balances with identified claimants or creditors.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to analyze the
noted transactions and make the necessary correcting entries pursuant to
Sections 59 and 61 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and furnish the Audit Team
for verification.

Henceforth, strictly adhere to the pro forma entries set forth by MNGAS for LGUs
Volume I for the cancellation of stale and cancelled checks.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

Enjoin the Inventory Committee to:

Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE); and

Expedite the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented in the financial statements;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary correcting entries for the erroneous depreciation;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Ledger Cards; and
Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare Property Cards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with all department heads and retrieve all supporting documents for unpaid liabilities of the Municipality that may be in their custody and validate the documents that may be gathered;
Send notices to the creditors affected for possible claim, and
If still not claimed within reasonable period of time, cause reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require its Local Development Council, in consultation with the Local Finance Committee, to update yearly its Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP) and ensure that all projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) as well as the subsequent reprogramming are among the listed projects in the LDIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineering Office in coordination with the Municipal Architect, for future similar projects, to prepare Program of Works to the extent that enable the projects operational.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive source from other appropriate funds the loan amortizations.
We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the unspent LDRRMF to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the HRMO to develop a standardized JO contract template that outlines, among others, the duration of employment, terms and conditions, specific duties and responsibilities, office assignments, rates, and work schedules and ensure that JO personnel signs the contract before starting work, with a copy provided to the employee.

We the Local Chief Executive to:

Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that The City Mayor direct the City Accounting and Internal Control Office (CAICO) to record purchases of goods, supplies and materials for distribution or consumption under appropriate inventory accounts, and record the issuances/expenses in the appropriate expenses account based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted by the City General Services Office (CGSO).
observations.managementAction: The CAICO started to use a system that will tag purchased goods, supplies and materials for distribution or consumption and recorded to its appropriate inventory/ expense accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Instruct the City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to ensure that PPAs included in the AIP are well-planned and implementation-ready, and with a specific period of implementation indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to achieve desirable socio-economic outcomes;
b. CPDO to undertake a comprehensive evaluation and reassessment whether the PPAs under continuing appropriations are still responsive to current constituents, readiness for procurement and implementation; and
c. City Engineering and Development Department (CEIDD) and the CPDO to strictly monitor the progress of work and facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO is committed to ensuring that the goals and objectives of PPAs are well-defined and aligned with the list of priority projects. The CPDO conducted an assessment and evaluation and reassessment of PPAs under continuing appropriations to deterimine its responsiveness to current needs and readiness for procurement and implementation. Likewise, the CEIDD and the CPDO enhanced the monitoring and implementation of the PPAs through planning and scheduling, including establishment of detailed timelines with specific milestones and deadlines to track progress and ensure timely completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. direct the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to update and reconcile their records to identify the semi-expendable reclassification for these items and prepare a separate schedule for these items; b. instruct the City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop in the PPE value items by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation; and c. require the City Accountant and the CGSO to prepare and maintain all the forms, registries and reports prescribed by COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties of the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and the Government of Calamba conducted the “One-Time PPE Cleansing of PPE accounts”, which includes the identification and reclassification of semi-expendable properties. The CGSO and the CAICO initiated the preparation and maintenance of forms and registries. However, the volume of PPE items to be reconciled for certain items is still in progress. The CGSO will maintain the same personnel in the Property and Supply Division, and discourage the frequent transfer of staff personnel, which affects the continuity of service. The integration of existing systems across four departments was implemented by the Accounting Office which enables the production of reports such as the PAR and PCs, among others. The CGSO, in collaboration with the CAICO, has recently updated the records that will support the production of reports such as the PAR and PCs. The CGSO will include a plan for storage/warehouse facilities for delivered supplies and materials. The City Government has required personnel concerned to reach the end of their contract, or transfer to different government offices to obtain clearance to monitor the movement of property. The CAICO has taken up the necessary accounting entry under JEV No. J000244 dated December 29, 2023 to write off/drop from books of accounts the remaining balances as of December 31, 2022 lumped under the “Beginning Balance” and “Unreconciled SL” which were created to facilitate the conversion of accounts from the old Government Accounting System to the New Government Accounting System (NGAS).
observations.reason: The reconciliation of Accounting and Property records for PPE remained pending for the Special Education Fund (SEF) due to problems encountered in the distribution of PPE items across multiple schools within the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. explain/justify the fund incurrence of the expenditures charged out of the previous years' unexpended LDRRMF and submit the necessary liquidation reports thereof; and b. comply with the regularity requirements and return unutilized funds to source NGAs or remit to National Treasury as necessary.
