Fund (GF) of P4,469,000.00, Medical Equipment of P21,683.24, and Technical
and Scientific Equipment P86,480.62 in the SEF;
• prepare PPELCs and reconcile with the Lapsing Schedule and identify the
PPES with under recording of Accumulated Depreciation and effect the
necessary adjusting entries; and
B. CGSO to:
• prepare the PCs.
• ensure that all issued PPEs are covered with PARs and renew these every
three years or as necessary.
C. CGSO and the CAICO to:
• complete the reconciliation of their records based on RPCPPE submitted by
the Inventory Committee and ensure that the PPELCs and PCs are reconciled;
and
• follow the procedures provided under Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006
for the missing School Building of P2,376,500.04, Office Equipment of
P21,195,226.18, ICT of P55,821,030.55, Communication Equipment of
P64,675.24, and Furniture and Fixtures of P27,939,970.22 all under the Special
Education Fund (SEF) totaling P107,397,402.23

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CEIDD to find ways to minimize/avoid
delays in project implementation by performing complete/adequate surveys and site
investigations to ensure that issues such as the right of ways problems, conflicts
with other ongoing projects, and resettlement and relocation of informal settlers,
among others, are solved/acted upon before proceeding to the bidding of the project.

We recommended that Management:
a. establish a well-defined policy/guidelines and procedures for the granting of
financial assistance, which should provide criteria for the selection of qualified
applicants/grantees, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary
requirements, and the like;
b. Leverage a computerized system to monitor the grant of financial assistance,
streamline processes, enhance transparency and ensure the equitable distribution
of limited resources to those in need.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. instruct the City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to ensure that PPAs
included in the AIP are well-planned and procurement and implementation ready
with aspecific period of implementation indicated in the AIP to achieve desirable
socio-economic targets and outcomes;
b. CPDO to undertake a comprehensive evaluation and reassessment whether the
PPAs under the continuing appropriations are still responsive to current needs of
the City’s constituents, otherwise, initiate their reprogramming to more necessary
and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects; and henceforth
c. City Engineering and Infrastructure Development Department (CEID)and/or the
CPDO to strictly monitor the progress of work and facilitate the timely
implementation of development projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Council (CDRRMC) to ensure that the allocation for disaster mitigation,
prevention, and preparedness provides details as to projects and activities to be
funded, including a list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF
of previous years.

We recommended that the City Mayordirect the CHRMO to:
a. develop a staffing pattern plan that outlines the necessary positions, skills, and
competencies required for each department/office;
b. draft and implement written policies and procedures for the engagement of
services of JO/COS based on the existing laws and regulations governing these
workers in the government; and
c. assess the performance/qualifications of the COS/JOs for the possibility of hiring
them as permanent employees to fill up the 215 vacant positions.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a resolution
institutionalizing the engagement of JO/COS services prepared by CHRMO upon
review and evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CAICO to coordinate with the City
Human Resource Management Office (CHRMO) to process and update the JO and
COS PhilHealth membership records.

Additionally, we recommended that the CAICO ensure all contributions to PhilHealth
are remitted in full within the prescribed period, following the New Payment Schedule
for Premium Contributions set forth by PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables from Calamba Water System subscribers and maintain subsidiary ledger for every account; and

For the subsequent issuance of Financial Statements, require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements relevant information regarding the Calamba Water System receivables.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; and

b. 	For the succeeding years, enjoin the Municipal Accountant to timely transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account.

We recommended that management:

Direct the municipal assessor to prepare a financial and operational plan and requirements to carry out the tax mapping; and

Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to support funding initiatives for the preparation of tax maps and subsequent related activities thereto.

We recommended that management:

Provide additional manpower in the BAC Office to assist the BAC Secretariat in all documentation requirements for procurement; and

Direct the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking supporting documents to facilitate auditorial review of the above presented projects. Henceforth, double check completeness of the attached supporting documents before submission to the Audit Team.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to submit the aforementioned lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team. Henceforth, ensure that documentation on infrastructure related disbursements is in accordance with COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management:

Require all JO workers to submit Accomplishment Reports and to quantify daily accomplishments in the reports; and

Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the delegated tasks to job order workers based on their accomplishments and assess their effectiveness and efficiency to determine reasonableness of their service.

We recommended that Management:

Require the separated employees to refund/return the excess terminal leave benefits received;

Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to properly review and update all the leave credits earned by the employees of the LGU in accordance with Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and attend appropriate seminars conducted by the Civil Service Commission; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements to the audit team, and henceforth ensure that disbursements are supported by requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that management enjoin the Local School Board to incorporate ECCD Programs in the programs, projects and activities appropriated for Special Education Fund pursuant to Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410.

We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Point System to attach the documents required in paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of JMC 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the: a. City Treasurer to update their records based on the updated assessment roll of the City Assessor and furnish the City Accountant with a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting up the receivable due for the year; and b. City Accountant to effect appropriate adjustments to the RPT/SET Receivables accounts and reconcile the records and address any discrepancies in receivable balances through necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer's Office (CTMO) has completed updating their records and has furnished the City Accounting and Internal Control Office (CAICO) with the updated assessment roll of the City Assessor and the latest records. The CAICO has been reconciling records and continues to reconcile the records with the reports from the Treasury Office regarding both the actual and estimated income of the City. This process was guided by the cited rulings, ensuring accuracy and compliance with established financial regulations.
observations.reason: The CAICO was unable to fully implement this audit recommendation due to the substantial volume of transactions within the City and insufficient staffing levels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. enjoin the City Accountant and the City General Services Office (CGSO) to reconcile their records as to the status and costs of the ongoing projects and make the necessary adjustments to the books of accounts; b. direct the CGSO and the City Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the discrepancy of P407,367,168.44 between GL and the RPCPPE by submitting a properly accomplished RPCPPE as of December 31, complete with data/information about the property to determine their existence, conditions and whereabouts; c. direct the CGSO to maintain complete Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a LRNPC for all roads and their components; d. City Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for every road and road component; and e. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and the CGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The City conducted the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts. The CAICO has taken up the accounting entry to write off/drop from books of accounts the “Beginning Balance” and “Unreconciled SL” as of December 31, 2022. The CGSO and the CAICO are working on reconciliation of records, and the CGSO has maintained Property Cards for roads and updated components. The CAICO manages the subsidiary ledger and lapsing schedule for road and road networks, updating records based on received disbursement vouchers. The City Engineer has furnished the City Accountant and the CGSO with comprehensive descriptions and cost breakdowns for road components within road projects. Despite these actions, variances still exist as of December 31, 2023.
observations.reason: A variance still exists between the General Ledger and the Reconciliation Tracking Sheet as of December 31, 2023; reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. enjoin the City Accountant and the City Engineer to reconcile their records as to the status and costs of the ongoing projects and effect the necessary adjustments in the respective records; and b. require the City Accountant to correctly classify the three projects with an aggregate cost of P29,255,661.72 by debiting CIP – Infrastructure Assets and crediting CIP – Land Improvements.
observations.managementAction: The CAICO reviewed the accounting ledger and discovered the difference amount of P6,118,000.00 represents adjustments for canceled checks due to delays in the release of their survey report, as indicated by the CTMO. The CAICO has correctly classified and reclassified the three projects with aggregate cost of P29,255,661.72 through JEV Nos. 101-2023-K000458 and 101-2023-J000036 dated March 20, 2023, and further adjustments have been made. The City Engineer has submitted termination notices for projects as requested by the City Accountant. Reconciliation activities and corrections have been undertaken.
observations.reason: Inaccuracies or inconsistencies in the recorded data led to discrepancies between billing records and reports; reconciliation continues but necessary accounting adjustments have been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to exert diligent effort to obtain from the bank the copies of the Credit Memos/Debit Memos as bases in recording the reconciling items totaling P20,514,070.93 to obtain the correct amount of Cash in Bank (CIB) accounts as presented in the financial statements; b. prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) regularly in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and c. consider opening a separate bank account for the online payment facility employed by the City for easy identification of transactions and tracing of individual accounts from source documents.
observations.managementAction: Bank reconciliations are being prepared regularly by the CAICO personnel in charge. The City Treasurer will ask for the endorsement of the Mayor’s Office to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass a resolution to open a separate bank account. The CAICO faces difficulty in distinguishing online payments from bank statements due to numerous entries; the CAICO is reconciling and updating records pending receipt of supporting documents from the depository bank.
observations.reason: The CAICO is awaiting supporting documents from the depository bank for unidentified online deposits; other urgent tasks have impacted the faster implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. CGSO, City Engineer and the City Accountant to reconcile their records as to the status and costs incurred related to ongoing projects and make necessary adjustments; b. CGSO to maintain a complete Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a LRNPC for all roads and their components; c. City Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for every road and road component; and d. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and the CGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer has furnished the City Accountant and the CGSO with complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components for road projects. The CAICO is managing the subsidiary ledger and lapsing schedule for road and road networks, updating records based on received disbursement vouchers. The CGSO maintained LRNPC for all roads and their respective components. The reconciliation effort has been initiated though some differences remain due to documentation timing and release of survey reports.
observations.reason: Delays in the validated survey report affected reconciliation; some records (canceled checks and supporting documentation) were processed later leading to timing differences.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. require the concerned offices/departments to make as part of their monthly process the delivery process the immediate submission of complete documents supporting the appropriate recording of the City's obligations in the books; and henceforth b. direct the City Accountant to ensure that liabilities are recognized when the goods and services are accepted or receipt of supplier bills are received.
observations.managementAction: The CAICO reviewed existing processes to facilitate prompt recording of liabilities upon acceptance of goods/services and supplier bills. They require complete supporting documents such as sales invoices, billing statements, Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IARs), and other similar documents before payment. Advances will not be permitted unless for emergency purchases. The CAICO has instituted procedures to ensure liabilities are recorded when goods/services and supplier bills are received.
observations.reason: Advance payments for emergency procurement are exceptions; internal control improvements have been instituted to ensure liabilities are timely recognized once supporting documents are complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to refrain from recording the said allowances to the “Internet Subscription Expenses” account and charge them to the appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The CAICO reviewed the transactions and determined that the allowances should not be recorded under Internet Subscription Expenses. The CAICO reclassified the said allowances to the appropriate account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Engineer to prepare the Statement of Time Elapsed showing the status of the project, and attach the same for the final billing with regards to the implementation with regard to the project and the delay incurred; and b. City Accountant and/or the City Engineer to submit approved variation orders or the imposition of LDs of at least one-tenth of one percent (0.1%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay and deduct/collect the same from any money due the Contractor, whichever is available to the Management.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer submitted the Statement of Time Elapsed and the final billing was prepared, including calculations for the total delay incurred. The City Engineer has implemented procedures to compute LDs and include such calculations in final billings. The CAICO and the City Engineer coordinate on approved variation orders and LD impositions.
observations.reason: Records and final billings including LD rates have been prepared; steps are being institutionalized to ensure enforcement of penalties and adjustments in final billing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Assessor to: a. prepare and submit updated SFMVs or general revision of property assessments which they plan to conduct in CY 2024 to reflect prevailing market prices and generate additional income from increased tax assessment; and b. submit to the City Human Resources and Management Office and City Administration Office a proposal for the reorganization of plantilla items to address workforce inadequacy specifically in the Tax Mapping Division and revise SFMVs every three years in compliance with Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor submitted the SFMV they plan to conduct in CY 2024 to the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF). The City Assessment Office (CAO) submitted a proposed reorganization staffing pattern to the City Human Resource Management Office (CHRMO). The CAO will ensure compliance with the recommendations and revise SFMVs every three years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. justify the preparation of the original CY 2022 Annual SEF Budget which was lesser compared with the actual proceeds received from the additional one percent tax on real property; and b. require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to continue the reconciliation of records to submit the actual and estimated income of the City and continue to reconcile records to ensure compliance with rules.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer adopted a conservative approach in estimating the Annual SEF Budget due to unpredictable collection rates and pandemic impacts. Tax amnesty programs affected gross collections. The CAICO has continued reconciling records with the Treasury Office reports and will continue to reconcile both actual and estimated income. The CPDO and CAICO ensured compliance with rulings and updated records accordingly.
observations.reason: The original budget was conservatively estimated due to uncertainties in collection rates during the pandemic; reconciliation of records is ongoing and has been guided by applicable rulings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineering and Infrastructure Development Department (CEIDD) and City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to ensure that the City's PPAs are implemented and completed in a timely manner through proper planning and scheduling of each procurement activity, including coordination with other implementing agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to ensuring goals and objectives of PPAs are well-defined and aligned with priority projects. The CPDO will enhance monitoring and implementation through proper planning and scheduling, establishment of detailed timelines with milestones and deadlines, and collaboration with CEIDD and other implementing agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a. evaluate and reassess the above projects under the continuing appropriations; b. fast track the completion of ongoing development projects and closely monitor implementation so problems can be immediately addressed; and c. intensify monitoring and evaluation controls in implementing the City's programs, projects and activities for their timely completion.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer and CPDO will provide updated status reports on projects under Continuing Appropriations. The CEIDD and CPDO are collaborating to ensure project goals and objectives are defined, align with priority projects, and to enhance monitoring to track progress and ensure timely completion. Resources will be allocated and frequent communication established to monitor project progress and address issues.
observations.reason: Monitoring and implementation enhancements are ongoing; some projects are being reassessed and reprogrammed as needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (CDRRM) to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by specifying the identified priority disaster management programs and projects in the original and supplemental LDRRMFIP and ensure proper inclusion and approval of funds used from previous years' balances.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that the expenditures of P3,764,460.00 were charged out of the previous years unexpended LDRRMF due to the urgent nature of the situation (supply and delivery of assorted food commodities for families affected by typhoon Paeng). It was overlooked or omitted to include the PPAs in the 2023 LDRRMFIP and steps will be taken to ensure future inclusions and compliance.
observations.reason: The expenditures were incurred due to urgent disaster response needs; omission to include them in the 2023 LDRRMFIP was an oversight and corrective measures will be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CHRMO to: a. develop a staffing pattern plan that outlines the necessary positions, skills, and competencies required for each department/office; b. draft and implement written policies and procedures for the engagement of services of JO/COS based on existing laws and regulations; and c. assess performance/qualifications of the COS/JOs for possibility of hiring them as permanent employees to fill up vacant positions. We also recommended the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a resolution institutionalizing engagement of JO/COS services prepared by CHRMO upon review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: CHRMO formulated policies governing hiring of JO/COS and submitted a draft policy for legal review by CLSO; the CHRMO is finalizing guidelines for Mayor's review and approval, expected by end of 1st Quarter 2024, and is developing a medium/long-term Human Resource Management and Development Plan (HRMDP). CHRMO is also pursuing PRIME-HRM initiatives and has suggested staffing modifications to the reorganization committee.
observations.reason: Initial phases of reorganization and policy finalization are ongoing; full institutionalization requires further consultations and completion of HRMDP activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustment for the unrecorded depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the depreciation that was not recorded and supported by a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the municipal accountant to draw a correcting entry for transactions misclassified under the AP account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant drawn the correcting entry for the misclassified transactions under the AP account.

observations.recommendation: b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the under-recording of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant Has drawn correcting entries treating the under-recording of RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period adjustments/errors by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable, and ensure adequate disclosures thereof in compliance with IPSAS 3.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has provided adequate disclosures for the prior period adjustment/error in the NTFS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Development Council through the MPDO to ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Management endeavored to implement all projects during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive rescind the contract with JDCO Phil. Builders, Inc. on the affected project; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has rescinded the contract thru letter dated August 17, 2023 and was received by the Contractor on August 31, 2023

observations.recommendation: 1. Instruct the LDRRM Council to include all the unexpended prior year balances in its LDRRMF Investment Plan;
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has passed and approved the Resolution Nos. 23 and 86, all series of 2023 amounting to P5,755,321.56 and P344,397.97, respectively, for the reprogramming of the unexpended DRRMF balances

observations.recommendation: 2. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to: i. Ensure the optimum utilization of the LDRRM Fund;
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, the Municipality registered 98% utilization rate on its Mitigation Fund, inclusive of Continuing fund.

observations.recommendation: 2. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to: ii. Refrain from charging unrelated programs, projects and activities of disaster risk reduction and management and adhere to the provisions of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;
observations.managementAction: Charges against the DRRMF were compliant to pertinent utilization guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the prospective suppliers to indicate brand names together with their relevant specifications during the filling out of the Requests for Quotations (RFQs) and ensure that brand names together with their complete detailed item specifications to be procured from the winning supplier are indicated in the Purchase Orders in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: Items stipulated in the Purchase Orders are sufficiently described with particularity, including brand names.

observations.recommendation: 1. Submit a written justification highlighting the necessity of conducting the ELA outside the locality when it could have been more efficiently undertaken within the locality; and
observations.managementAction: The LGU has submitted written justification and the Audit Team has found it sufficiently convincing and justified.

observations.recommendation: 2. Enjoin the Local Chief Executive to strongly advocate to the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the geographical island where the municipality is located and in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.
observations.managementAction: The LGU, thru the Local Chief Executive, has assured that they will measures to use LGU fund with due prudence and diligence.

observations.recommendation: (a) create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy-making;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, through the GAD Focal Point Persons, had already created a GAD Database, which included gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy-making.

observations.recommendation: (b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal person had submitted their GAD Database on time.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the department concerned to submit the list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office submitted the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs

observations.recommendation: Inform the audit team within ten (10) days after award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the project signboard and/or public notices are posted; and

Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of TCT to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for Land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value.
observations.managementAction: Insofar as the titles properties are concerned, the Municipal Assessor has already provided copies of the TCT to the Municipal accountant.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management: Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.
observations.managementAction: The RHU Inventory Incharge submitted a monthly inventory report as well as the report of issuances to the Municipal Accounting Office

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoin collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has 24/7 operation center, procured heavy equipment, and complied with the basic disaster preparedness standards. The MDRRMO meets the basic level of disaster preparedness.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the concerned personnel to attach the approved payrolls or list of payees indicating their net payments in the Disbursement Vouchers for cash advances granted to the DO as basis for the concerned Municipal Officials in certifying and approving the disbursements.

We have recommended that management direct the concerned officials to immediately settle their accountabilities through the submission of necessary supporting documents and to refund any excess amount of cash advances. Otherwise, withhold the salaries of concerned municipal officials and employees until they shall have complied with the requirement of this Commission in accordance with Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office prepare a working paper describing the assets recorded in the PPE account with an acquisition cost of less than P15,000.00, including the supporting documents such as the Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) of said assets and other relevant data.  Draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in order to make an adjusting entry to remove the same from the account “Property, Plant, and Equipment”. All these will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification; and
Subsequent purchases of equipment and other items with acquisition cost of less than P15,000.00 shall  be recorded as expense upon issuance with the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to establish accountability over assets.

We have recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Engineer, the BAC and other concerned personnel the immediate submission of the documents to avoid further delay in the conduct of technical review and inspection of the LGU’s projects.  Moreover, ensure that documents submitted for contract review shall be complete.

We have recommended that management require the Accounting Office to immediately submit the 1,076 DVs with complete supporting documents and to adhere the prescribed timeline in the submission of paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor concerned pursuant to COA rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management:

Require Southwestern Pension House and ML Descallar Corporation to submit the request for payment including the detailed statement of account showing the names of quarantined individuals and date/number of days quarantined and daily rental rate; and

Clearly indicate the rental rates on the MOA for future similar transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management already attached to the disbursement vouchers the request for payment including the detailed statement of account showing the names of quarantined individuals and date/number of days quarantined and daily rental rate.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality in accordance with Section II-A.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO made a consolidated list of donations received and distributed by the LGU and submitted copy to COA.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

Organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC;

Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management; and

Confer with DSWD – Regional office No. 10 on the results of the revalidation and obtain a written advise as to the course of action if recipients were declared to be ineligible.
observations.managementAction: The management organized a validation team who work closely in coordinating with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to verify on the eligibility of the aforesaid beneficiaries. A written report was made containing the findings, remarks and justification of the conducted verification activities. The written report was then submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct all concerned officials to provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement activities in order to determine the specific requirements of the end users and clearly define the deliverables of the supplier.
observations.managementAction: The management immediately directed all departments to provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement activities. Thus, Purchase Request is subject to checking upon receiving to the BAC in order to correct the mispractices of using unclear and ambiguous terms in the Purchase Order, Purchase Request, and Request for Quotation.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management  require the BAC to observe strict compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The management required the BAC to strictly observe compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management effect the necessary increase of the fidelity bond of the Accountable Officers commensurate to their actual accountabilities, in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.
observations.managementAction: The management took effect the increase of fidelity bond of Accountable Officers upon its renewal to the Bureau of Treasury. The same action will be taken to other Accountable Officers whose fidelity bond is subject to renewal and of which increase is necessary.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management effect the payment of goods and services through check payable to the supplier or dealer except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check and stop paying thru cash advance for predetermined expenditures where direct payment to suppliers is possible.
observations.managementAction: The management informed all department heads that there will be no more cash advances for predetermined expenditure and advised them to pay goods and services through a check payable to the supplier or dealer except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management:

Instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure and commence the processing of the TCT of all the land properties, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and

Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of TCT to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for Land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value.
observations.managementAction:
Three land properties were donated to Calamba Central School and Libertad Nursery.  Application for the market is already submitted while the Landfill and Cemetery are processed for application. For other remaining land properties, the LGU is still collecting the required documents for the application of land title.

The agency has difficulty in securing relevant documents in order to process the titling of land.
observations.reason:
A TCT has been secured for one land property while the titles of other properties are still being processed. 

Meanwhile, the Market Values are already updated as of 2018 as approved by the Sangguniang
Panlalawigan.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that management:

Conduct actual physical inventory-taking of the properties and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and

Require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management created a Municipal Inventory Committee and conducted physical inventory count for one time cleansing of inventories and required the use of Property Cards and Ledger Cards. After the actual physical inventory reconciliation of the balances will follow for the fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.reason: Inventory taking has been done already however the same was not yet reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies as inventory;

Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report
 of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and

Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.
observations.managementAction: The RHU Inventory In-charge submitted a monthly inventory report as well as report of issuances of prior year 2020 to the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Purchases of drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies are still recorded as outright expense.

observations.managementAction: The concerned department offices already submitted the contracts and its supporting documents and some of the lacking documents enumerated in the mentioned in the audit finding.
observations.reason: Supporting documents for various projects were still not submitted as discussed in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has demanded a duly complied lease contract with an approved Requisition Slip as a requirement for the rental payments and logbooks are maintained to record the activities of the heavy equipment. A memorandum was issued and duly received by the municipal collectors to expedite an effective and efficient internal control system.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Pursuant to COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054, submit justification that the honoraria granted to the Municipal Board of Canvassers during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections is not similar to the honorarium or allowance given by the COMELEC, otherwise the payment shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already submitted a justification that the honoraria granted to the Municipal Board of Canvassers during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections is not similar to the honorarium or allowance given by the COMELEC
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoin collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.
observations.managementAction: Some deficiencies are already fully implemented such as the 24/7 operation center and purchase of heavy equipment. The MDRRMO continues to fully implement the other deficiencies by incorporating such in the investment plan and almost in compliance with the minimum standards for disaster preparedness.
observations.reason:
The agency continued its effort in complying all the requirements to be fully compliant to the minimum standard for disaster preparedness. As for this year, the agency was able to create couples/
conjugal room, space for livestock’s is provided, laundry space is also made available in the evacuation center.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. City GSO to prepare and submit the RPCPPE by PPE account and by
department/office, to facilitate reconciliation of PPE account balances with
accounting records, in conformity with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I;
b. City GSO to maintain Property Cards and reconcile them with the accounting
ledgers for proper monitoring, in accordance with Paragraphs 3 and 4 of
Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. City Accountant to provide depreciation with lapsing schedules for the Flood
Control System and Power Supply System accounts totaling P18,698,478.10
under the SEF, pursuant to the provision of IPSAS 17; and
d. City Accountant and the City GSO exert extra effort to determine the details
for the unidentified PPE items totaling P231,465,671.90 following the
guidelines and procedures of the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts” as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. strictly enforce the liquidation of outstanding CAs by the SDO totaling
P50,354,050.00 supported with complete documents;
b. direct the City Accountant to refrain from granting additional CAs to officials,
officers or employees without settling first their previous CAs;
c. establish a centralized database of beneficiaries installed in the Office of the
City Social Welfare and Development Officer and its sub-divisions, to facilitate
delivery of its social services program efficiently and effectively; and
henceforth,
d. strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of CAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. exert diligent effort to obtain from the bank, in coordination with the Acting
City Treasurer, the bank credit/debit advices to support the credit and debit
memo identified in the BRS as bases for the necessary adjustments in the
books;
b. trace the unrecorded deposits and withdrawals by comparing the validated
deposit slips and the Accountant’s advice duly acknowledged by the bank
against the bank statements, Report of Checks Issued and the subsidiary
ledgers and draw the necessary adjusting entries; and henceforth,
c. prepare the BRS regularly, and promptly adjust valid reconciling items, to
reflect the correct cash balances in bank accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. recognize liability only when goods and services are accepted or rendered
and supplier/creditor bills are received; and
b. determine the validity of the unliquidated obligations of P4,660,511.41, which
are outstanding for more than three years, and if found to have no legitimate
claim against the City, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the
GF.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:
a. require the Contractor to engage the catch-up program in order to fast track
the completion of the project to avoid rescindment of the contract;
b. ensure that all contract extensions are covered by performance security until
the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the City to guarantee
the faithful performance of the Contractor;
c. take appropriate actions for the delay in the completion of the project by
imposing liquidated damages of at least one-tenth of one percent (0.1%) of
the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay;
d. intensify monitoring of the project in order to avoid recurrence of delay to
other projects; and henceforth,
e. conscientiously carry out the detailed engineering before the awarding of
contract and implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that issues
such as the viability of the construction sites are properly addressed during
the feasibility or preliminary engineering study prior to project
implementation to ensure timely completion of the projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:
a. provide written justifications for the delayed/non-implementation of the 14
infrastructure projects totaling P87,451,394.54 indicating valid reasons for the
non-performance of work in CY 2021, action taken to address the cause of
their suspension and updated status of the project;
b. submit approved written request of the Contractor for extension of contract
time, otherwise, impose liquidated damages on the Contractors for every day
of delay and initiate termination/rescission of the contract on infrastructure
projects when liquidated damages reach 10 per cent and negative slippages
of more than 15 per cent arising from the Contractor’s fault or negligence
based on the computation of the City Accounting Office; and henceforth,
c. ensure that detailed engineering activities are sufficiently carried out on the
basis of feasibility or preliminary engineering study made, which establishes
the technical viability of the project and conformance to land use and zoning
guidelines, before the awarding of the contract to immediately address issues
that will hamper their timely completion.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Acting City Treasurer to identify
the specific sources of the funds and determine whether these were idle funds in
accordance with COA Circular No. 92-382. If proven that these funds were indeed
“idle” within the context of the definition provided in the Circular, secure
authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod and approval of the Local Chief
Executive.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Local School Board to close or
terminate the time deposit accounts totaling P356,142,316.28 in the SEF, and use
the funds instead, in accordance with prioritized programs as provided under
Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4.1 DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Market Management Committee
to:
a. devise a more effective strategy or program to encourage interested vendors
or residents of the City to occupy the 545 vacant stalls, thereby increasing the
market collections;
b. initiate legal remedies on all stallholders without Contract of Lease and
execute a new binding contract declaring new terms and conditions for
stallholders/vendors to properly enforce collections, otherwise, consult with a
legal officer for the rescindment of contracts of lease still under the 1995 BOT
scheme for non-operating stalls;
c. formulate a flexible payment scheme for the delinquent vendors, such as the
waiving of surcharges and penalties or installment method, to compensate for
the increasing amount of outstanding market fees; and
d. revisit the existing Market Code of the City Government of Calamba and
update/revise the same, pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to maximize
its revenue-raising power by providing updated sanctions or penalties for
failure or non-payment of the monthly market and/or maintenance fee within
the prescribed period, and henceforth, ensure strict enforcement to increase
annual market collections.

We recommended that Management update/strengthen the existing Revenue
Code of the City Government, pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to fully
maximize its revenue-raising power, thus providing more funds for its operations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CPDO to:
a. conduct a thorough study/investigation and survey of areas/locations of the
proposed projects, in coordination with the barangays and other concerned
officials, prior to inclusion of the indicative PPAs in the City’s AIP under the
20 per cent DF to guarantee the full implementation of the PPAs in the
projected/scheduled year/date to be completed; and
b. evaluate and reassess the PPAs under the continuing appropriations if these
PPAs are still responsive to the current needs of their constituents, otherwise,
initiate reprogramming of PPAs to more viable and significant PPAs to meet
the socio-economic needs of the City.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to fast-track
the ongoing development projects and closely monitor their implementation so
that any problems can be immediately addressed.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineer to provide written justifications to the non-implementation of
the LDRRMF projects totaling P49,906,330.95, indicating valid reasons for the
non-performance of work in CY 2021, action taken to address the cause of
their suspension and updated status of the projects, and henceforth, ensure
that detailed engineering activities are sufficiently carried out before the
awarding of contract and implementation of infrastructure projects to
immediately address issues which will hamper their timely completion; and
b. City Planning and Development Coordinator and Officer-in-Charge (OIC)-City
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to closely
monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects,
and activities related to disaster preparedness and mitigation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. City Treasurer to immediately furnish the City Accountant with all credit and debit memos received from LBP, deposits made on all collections, especially those deposits made at the end of the month, and other documents that will affect the cash balances at month-end; and
b. City Accountant to record in the books of account all reconciling items found to establish the correctness of monthly and year-end cash balances.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer furnished the City Accountant with all the credit and debit memos. The City Accountant recorded in the books the reconciling items.
observations.reason: Unrecorded year-end bank debit/credit memos still exist.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. Acting City Treasurer to secure from the City Assessor an updated assessment roll as basis in the preparation of the certified lists showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible real property taxes for the year, which includes delinquent taxpayers and furnish the City Treasurer’s Office; and
b. City Accountant to: b.1 prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the updated and certified lists; and b.2 reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables prepared with the Quarterly Report on Real Property Tax Collections and certified lists of tax delinquencies as of year-end and make necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor submitted the updated assessment roll to the City Treasurer’s Office. The City Accounting Office prepared the necessary adjusting entries and reconciled the Receivables prepared.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer’s Office is still updating the CY Certified List of Taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Cooperative and Livelihood Development Officer (CCLDO) to set up the corresponding Allowance for Impairment Loss in the books of accounts based on the collectability and assessment by the CCLDO of the long outstanding loan receivables; b. derecognize the asset (Loan Receivable account) by requesting authority to write-off to be forwarded to the COA, through proper channels, guided with the provisions under COA Circular No. 2016-005, if the receivables have been proven to be uncollectible and inactive/non-moving in the books of accounts for ten years or more; and c. review/analyze the discrepancy of P4.11 between the balances of the loans receivables per financial statement of OCA against the status report of CCLDD and draw Journal Entry Voucher to take up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: Allowance for Impairment Loss was already set up in the books with JEV No. 2022-101-J000003. Request to write-off was not prepared. Analysis of the accounts was made and the difference was due to erroneous classification and the same was already adjusted in 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all PPE accounts to determine the existence and completeness of the PPE owned by the City, prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS-LGUs; b. City Accountant and the CGSO to: b.1 reconcile the amount of PPE per Accounting records with the RPCPPE to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE account balances appearing in the statements; b.2 verify, analyze and evaluate the causes of discrepancies, if any; b.3 make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the book of accounts for the discrepancies noted; and c. CGSO to include in the RPCPPE the condition of all property of the City and, if found unserviceable, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other necessary documents for proper disposal of these property items.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO admitted that the COVID-19 pandemic affected physical inventory and preparation of complete and updated Property Cards. The CGSO requested each department/office to designate Inventory Officers who shall manage the inventory of these property items to help them identify and determine their conditions, and submitted the RPCPPE dated March 21, 2022.
observations.reason: Physical inventory was hampered due to COVID-19 pandemic situation; reconciliation and verification of discrepancies ongoing; preparation of complete and updated property cards is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City General Services Officer to: a.1 maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads and their components; a.2 keep a Local Road Network map; a.3 render a complete Report on Local Road Networks (LRN) at the end of the year; b. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the City Engineering Office, to conduct physical inventory of all existing LRNs in the City, prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network to be reconciled in the accounting records and LRNPC, and furnish a copy of the inventory report to the Audit Team for verification and audit; c. City Accountant to: c.1 identify the road lots recorded under the Land account and effect their reclassification to the Road Networks account; c.2 reconcile the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry in Public Infrastructure; c.3 keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network; c.4 segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost; c.5 prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and c.6 disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and d. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO now maintains a complete Local Road inventory and LRNPC. The CGSO submitted the Report on LRN. The Accounting Office identified the road lots under the Land account and recorded the necessary adjustments. The Accounting Office prepared a lapsing schedule for the computation of yearly depreciation. The City Engineer submitted a report to the Accounting Office and CGSO providing the complete description of each LRN.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineer to submit to COA the Quarterly Reports on Government PPAs with complete details as basis for recording/adjusting/recognize/classify the completed projects to its appropriate asset account; and
b. City Accountant to: b.1 reconcile the ledger balances of the CIP accounts with the Quarterly Report on PPAs submitted by the CEIDO and record the necessary adjustments, if any; and b.2 draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the appropriate asset account/s the completed projects totaling P1,497,734.18 and record the corresponding provision for depreciation; and adjust/correct the account Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets.
observations.managementAction: GPPAs were submitted to the Audit Team. The Accounting Office reconciled the ledger balances with the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs and prepared JEV to recognize the completed projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant, in coordination with the Acting City Treasurer, to reconcile the outstanding balance of Loans Payable – Domestic with the balance presented in the financial statements with the balance in the Annual Statement of Indebtedness, Payments and Balances prepared by the City Treasury Management Office, and prepare all necessary adjusting/correcting entries; and
b. City Accountant to classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable in the Statement of Financial Position by re-computing the current and non-current portions of the loan payable based on the Amortization Schedule provided by the Bank and prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: The outstanding balance in the Annual SIPB of the treasury was reconciled with the balance presented in the FS. The City Accountant classified the balance of the long-term loans payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. exert effort to retrieve the documents to support the dormant accounts; b. prepare the necessary correcting/adjusting entries on all misclassified accounts and error in the recording of prior years’ income; and c. coordinate with the City Treasurer and City Engineer for possible confirmation from various contractors with the status of the outstanding retention money and/or the possible reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the 10 percent retention money totaling P4,355,925.86, which remained outstanding for two to five years.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office confirmed that the claims for the surveying and registration services, rental of the venue, and repair of the Isuzu wrecker totaling P2,698,227.21 were invalid, thus reverted the aforementioned amount to Unappropriated Surplus of the GF. They are waiting for status of transaction from Prowater Technologies amounting to P1,695,000.00 from the City Engineering Office. The transactions from Alghen Trading and Julian and Christian Enterprises totaling P267,284.20 are in process of payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. determine the amount of fund transfers from the GF Proper to Special Accounts in the GF, particularly in regard to Calamba City College, to ascertain the adjusted Cash balance as of December 31, 2020 and draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the necessary correcting entries; b. see to it that fund transfer from the General Fund Proper to Special Accounts in the GF, and vice versa, is recorded every time there is a transaction involved therein; c. prepare financial statements for each special account in the GF so that special account Management may be adequately informed on the result of its operation for appropriate action and submit the same to the Audit Team; and d. maintain special accounts for Cemetery operations in compliance with the aforementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office drew JEV to correct the GF Proper and Special Accounts. Separate financial statements are prepared for each special account. The cemetery is not operating; future collections will be recorded as regular collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to immediately submit to the Auditor the 42 DVs and their supporting documents to ascertain the validity, legality and propriety of transactions and henceforth require submission of accounts within the first 10 days of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: On May 12, 2021, the City submitted to the Audit Team the 42 DVs and five (5) DVs on March 10, 2021, and three (3) DVs on March 23, 2022. The City will require submission of accounts within first 10 days of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.reason: Not all disbursement vouchers were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, as the Chairman of the Local School Board: a. require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to submit the actual and estimated income under the provisions of 5.5 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017; b. ensure that the appropriations/budgets for the proposed Programs, Activities, Projects (PAPs) included in the Annual and Supplemental Investment Plan (SIP) are aligned with the net amount available for appropriation as shown in SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1; c. present the SEF budget by program, activity, project, allotment class and per school as required under 5.2.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1; and d. see to it that the proposed PPAs included in the AIP and SIP are compliant with the policies on the use of SEF to support the needs of the schools and alternative learning modalities due to COVID-19 thereby, efficient and maximum utilization of SEF can be achieved.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer submitted the report to the LSB. The LSB prepared the SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1. The LSB has provided the AIP and proposed PPAs to be implemented.
observations.reason: Only 68.74% of the CY 2021 budget was utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to reconcile the balances of their records pertaining to Cash in Bank-Local Currency account and immediately effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: The City
observations.reason: The observation on unrecorded cash reconciling items was discussed/reiterated under Observation No. 1, pages 45 to 46 under Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Acting City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in the preparation of the certified lists showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible real property taxes for the year which includes delinquent taxpayers and furnish a copy of the same to the City Accountant; b. City Accountant to: b.1 prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the updated and certified lists showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible real property taxes submitted by the Acting City Treasurer; and b.2 reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts, as well as the total collections for CY 2020 and succeeding years recorded in the accounting ledger, with the City Treasurer’s Quarterly Report on Real Property Tax Collections and certified lists of tax delinquencies as at year-end and make necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The City Government Officials concerned are amenable to the recommendations of the Audit Team. The City Assessor’s Office (CAO) will continue to perform ocular field inspection in 54 barangays of the City in order to update the data on all real properties. In addition, the CAO is currently in the transition phase from manual to computerized system on the transmittal of the assessment roll to the City Treasurer’s Office. Both the City Assessor and the Acting City Treasurer agreed to work diligently to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory items and record as expenses only those items actually expended/consumed during the accounting period; b) the CGSO to conduct physical count of inventories semestrally, generate/prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and c) the City Mayor instruct: a. the City Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock which is to be reconciled with the GL controlling accounts; b. the CGSO to properly maintain and observe correct recording in the SC for each type of inventory; record all receipts and issuances of supplies in the stock cards; consolidate all Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the City Accounting Office; and reconcile the Stock Cards periodically with the ledger cards of Office of the City Accountant; c. the City Accountant and the Supply Officer to reconcile the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories with the GL controlling accounts; and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO explained that all supplies encoded under Expense accounts were received and issued on the same day, hence, no inventory items were recorded. All deliveries and issuances of the same day were included in the weekly submission of SSMIs to the Accounting Office. Stock cards in the form of soft copy were also maintained and will be updated once the Accounting Office maintain their subsidiary ledger cards for reference. Since the CGSO warehouse facility has no capacity to house all supplies and materials delivered, only GSO supplies remained in the warehouse. Furthermore, COVID-19 pandemic refrain the CGSO from doing the annual inventory however, they were deliberately reaching out to offices to update the inventory and reconcile their records.
observations.reason: The City Accountant was unable to fully implement the recommendation due to volume of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: a. City General Services Officer to: a.1 maintain a complete Local Road Inventory and Road Map; a.2 keep a Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads and their components; and a.3 render a complete Report on Local Road Networks at the end of the year. b. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the City Engineering Office, to conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the City, prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network to be reconciled in the accounting records and LRNPC, and furnish a copy of the inventory report to the Audit Team for verification and audit; c. City Accountant to: c.1 reconcile the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry in Public Infrastructure; c.2 keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network; c.3 segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost; c.4 prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and c.5 disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008. d. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The City Government Officials concerned are amenable to the recommendations of the Audit Team.
